name: Moose Charlap
abstract: Morris Isaac "Moose" Charlap (né Charlip; December 19, 1928 – July 8, 1974) was an American Broadway composer best known for Peter Pan (1954), for which Carolyn Leigh wrote the lyrics. The idea for the show came from Jerome Robbins, who planned to have a few songs by Charlap and Leigh.

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Gilmore
about_quotes.evidence: his father was Moose Charlap, who wrote some Broadway shows, most notably “Peter Pan” I believe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Gilmore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999
